prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that compares three activation functions: ReLU, sigmoid, and tanh. Show them on separate miniature axes with labels and a short caption about nonlinearity.
completion:
from manim import *

class ActivationComparisonPanel(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Activation Functions", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        axes_group = VGroup()
        specs = [
            ("ReLU", BLUE_D, lambda x: max(0, x)),
            ("Sigmoid", GREEN_D, lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x))),
            ("tanh", ORANGE, lambda x: np.tanh(x)),
        ]
        for label, color, fn in specs:
            axes = Axes(
                x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
                y_range=[-1.2, 1.2, 1],
                x_length=2.9,
                y_length=2.2,
                tips=False,
                axis_config={"stroke_width": 2},
            )
            graph = axes.plot(fn, x_range=[-3, 3], color=color)
            title_label = Text(label, font_size=22, color=color).next_to(axes, UP, buff=0.2)
            axes_group.add(VGroup(axes, graph, title_label))
        axes_group.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.65).shift(UP * 0.2)
        caption = Text("Nonlinear activations shape how networks learn patterns.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(group[0], shift=UP * 0.2) for group in axes_group], lag_ratio=0.2), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(group[1]) for group in axes_group], lag_ratio=0.3), FadeIn(VGroup(*[group[2] for group in axes_group])), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that explains a residual block. Show an input block passing through two layers, a skip connection arc, and a merge node before the output.
completion:
from manim import *

class ResidualBlockFlow(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Residual Block", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        input_box = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.15, width=1.3, height=0.8, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.15).shift(LEFT * 4)
        layer1 = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.15, width=1.4, height=0.9, color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.15).shift(LEFT * 1.3)
        layer2 = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.15, width=1.4, height=0.9, color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.15).shift(RIGHT * 1.3)
        add_circle = Circle(radius=0.32, color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.12).shift(RIGHT * 3.4)
        plus = MathTex("+").move_to(add_circle)
        output_box = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.15, width=1.4, height=0.85, color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.15).shift(RIGHT * 5.2)
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("Input", font_size=22).move_to(input_box),
            Text("Layer 1", font_size=22).move_to(layer1),
            Text("Layer 2", font_size=22).move_to(layer2),
            Text("Output", font_size=22).move_to(output_box),
        )
        arrows = VGroup(
            Arrow(input_box.get_right(), layer1.get_left(), buff=0.15, color=GRAY_B),
            Arrow(layer1.get_right(), layer2.get_left(), buff=0.15, color=GRAY_B),
            Arrow(layer2.get_right(), add_circle.get_left(), buff=0.1, color=GRAY_B),
            Arrow(add_circle.get_right(), output_box.get_left(), buff=0.15, color=GRAY_B),
        )
        skip = VMobject(color=PURPLE_B, stroke_width=4)
        skip.set_points_smoothly([
            input_box.get_top() + UP * 0.05,
            input_box.get_top() + UP * 1.5 + RIGHT * 2.0,
            add_circle.get_top() + UP * 0.6 + LEFT * 0.4,
            add_circle.get_top(),
        ])
        note = Text("Skip connections preserve information and stabilize deep models.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(input_box), FadeIn(labels[0]), run_time=2)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowArrow(arrow) for arrow in arrows], lag_ratio=0.2), FadeIn(layer1), FadeIn(layer2), FadeIn(add_circle), FadeIn(plus), FadeIn(output_box), FadeIn(labels[1:]), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(skip), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(note), Indicate(skip, color=PURPLE_A), Indicate(add_circle, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that explains convolutional feature maps. Start with an input image grid, then show several stacked feature maps, a pooling stage, and a final compressed representation with arrows and labels.
completion:
from manim import *

class FeatureMapStack(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Convolutional Feature Maps", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        input_grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.22, stroke_width=1, fill_color=BLUE_B, fill_opacity=0.38)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(5, 5, buff=0.03).move_to(LEFT * 5)
        conv_stack = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=1.1, height=1.7, stroke_color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_C, fill_opacity=0.18).shift(RIGHT * 0.14 * i + UP * 0.08 * i)
            for i in range(4)
        ]).move_to(LEFT * 1.8)
        pool_stack = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=0.85, height=1.25, stroke_color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_C, fill_opacity=0.18).shift(RIGHT * 0.11 * i + UP * 0.07 * i)
            for i in range(3)
        ]).move_to(RIGHT * 1.0)
        embedding = VGroup(*[
            RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.08, width=0.55, height=0.28, color=PURPLE_D, fill_opacity=0.2).shift(DOWN * 0.34 * i)
            for i in range(5)
        ]).move_to(RIGHT * 4.3)
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("Input image", font_size=24).next_to(input_grid, DOWN),
            Text("Conv features", font_size=24).next_to(conv_stack, DOWN),
            Text("Pooling", font_size=24).next_to(pool_stack, DOWN),
            Text("Embedding", font_size=24).next_to(embedding, DOWN),
        )
        arrows = VGroup(
            Arrow(input_grid.get_right(), conv_stack.get_left(), buff=0.18, color=GRAY_B),
            Arrow(conv_stack.get_right(), pool_stack.get_left(), buff=0.18, color=GRAY_B),
            Arrow(pool_stack.get_right(), embedding.get_left(), buff=0.18, color=GRAY_B),
        )
        detail_box = SurroundingRectangle(conv_stack, color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        detail_text = Text("Each map responds to a different learned pattern.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(input_grid), FadeIn(labels[0]), run_time=2)
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrows[0]), FadeIn(conv_stack), FadeIn(labels[1]), run_time=3)
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrows[1]), FadeIn(pool_stack), FadeIn(labels[2]), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrows[2]), FadeIn(embedding), FadeIn(labels[3]), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(Create(detail_box), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(detail_text), Indicate(conv_stack, color=TEAL_A), Indicate(pool_stack, color=GREEN_A), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeOut(detail_box), run_time=1)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that visualizes latent-space interpolation. Show two endpoint vectors, a curved latent path between them, several intermediate sample panels, and a caption that the representation changes smoothly.
completion:
from manim import *

class LatentSpaceInterpolationPath(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Latent Space Interpolation", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], x_length=6.5, y_length=4.2, background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.22}).shift(LEFT * 2.6)
        point_a = Dot(plane.c2p(-2.5, -1.2), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.09)
        point_b = Dot(plane.c2p(2.4, 1.4), color=GREEN_D, radius=0.09)
        label_a = Text("z1", font_size=24, color=BLUE_D).next_to(point_a, DOWN)
        label_b = Text("z2", font_size=24, color=GREEN_D).next_to(point_b, UP)
        path = CubicBezier(
            point_a.get_center(),
            plane.c2p(-1.0, 2.0),
            plane.c2p(1.2, -2.0),
            point_b.get_center(),
            color=YELLOW_D,
        )
        sample_cards = VGroup()
        colors = [BLUE_E, TEAL_E, GREEN_E, YELLOW_E]
        for i, color in enumerate(colors):
            card = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, width=1.2, height=1.6, color=color, fill_opacity=0.12)
            bars = VGroup(*[
                Rectangle(width=0.16, height=0.25 + 0.18 * ((j + i) % 4), color=color, fill_opacity=0.65, stroke_width=0)
                for j in range(4)
            ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.08)
            bars.move_to(card)
            sample_cards.add(VGroup(card, bars))
        sample_cards.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.25).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        connector = Arrow(path.get_right(), sample_cards.get_left(), buff=0.2, color=GRAY_B)
        caption = Text("Smooth moves in latent space should produce smooth output changes.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(point_a), FadeIn(point_b), FadeIn(label_a), FadeIn(label_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(path), run_time=3)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(point_a.copy().set_color(YELLOW_A), path), run_time=3)
        self.play(GrowArrow(connector), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(card) for card in sample_cards], lag_ratio=0.25), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(sample_cards, color=TEAL_A), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that introduces a simplified benzene ring. Show a hexagonal carbon skeleton, alternating double bonds, and labels for several carbon and hydrogen positions.
completion:
from manim import *

class BenzeneRingLabels(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Benzene Ring", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        hexagon = RegularPolygon(n=6, radius=1.6, color=BLUE_D)
        carbon_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text("C", font_size=24).move_to(hexagon.point_from_proportion(i / 6) * 0.82)
            for i in range(6)
        ])
        bonds = VGroup()
        vertices = [hexagon.point_from_proportion(i / 6) for i in range(6)]
        for i in [0, 2, 4]:
            start = vertices[i]
            end = vertices[(i + 1) % 6]
            offset = 0.08 * normalize(rotate_vector(end - start, PI / 2))
            bonds.add(Line(start + offset, end + offset, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4))
        hydrogen_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text("H", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C).move_to(vertex * 1.28)
            for vertex in vertices
        ])
        note = Text("Alternating bonds capture the familiar benzene motif.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_C).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(hexagon), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(carbon_labels), FadeIn(hydrogen_labels), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(bond) for bond in bonds], lag_ratio=0.2), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(note), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that explains atomic radius trends across period 2. Show labeled circles for Li through Ne that shrink across the row and include a caption about increasing nuclear pull.
completion:
from manim import *

class AtomicRadiusTrend(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Atomic Radius Across Period 2", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        symbols = ["Li", "Be", "B", "C", "N", "O", "F", "Ne"]
        radii = [0.72, 0.65, 0.58, 0.52, 0.47, 0.43, 0.39, 0.35]
        circles = VGroup()
        for symbol, radius in zip(symbols, radii):
            circle = Circle(radius=radius, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_B, fill_opacity=0.18)
            label = Text(symbol, font_size=22).move_to(circle)
            group = VGroup(circle, label)
            circles.add(group)
        circles.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.38).shift(UP * 0.4)
        trend_arrow = Arrow(circles[0].get_bottom() + DOWN * 0.5, circles[-1].get_bottom() + DOWN * 0.5, color=YELLOW_D, buff=0)
        trend_text = Text("Radius decreases", font_size=24, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(trend_arrow, DOWN)
        caption = VGroup(
            Text("Across a period, effective nuclear charge increases.", font_size=24),
            Text("Electrons are pulled closer to the nucleus.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C),
        ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(group) for group in circles], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(trend_arrow), FadeIn(trend_text), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Indicate(group[0], color=TEAL_A) for group in circles], lag_ratio=0.15), run_time=5)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that shows a simple electron excitation in a Bohr-like atom. Animate one electron moving from an inner orbit to an outer orbit and add an arrow for absorbed energy.
completion:
from manim import *

class ElectronExcitation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Electron Excitation", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        nucleus = Circle(radius=0.48, stroke_color=RED_D, fill_color=RED_E, fill_opacity=0.32)
        shell_inner = Circle(radius=1.25, color=BLUE_C)
        shell_outer = Circle(radius=2.15, color=TEAL_C)
        electron = Dot(shell_inner.point_at_angle(PI / 4), radius=0.08, color=YELLOW)
        static_electron = Dot(shell_outer.point_at_angle(5 * PI / 4), radius=0.08, color=YELLOW)
        energy_arrow = Arrow(LEFT * 3.2 + DOWN * 1.6, LEFT * 1.4 + DOWN * 0.25, color=ORANGE, buff=0)
        energy_text = Text("energy in", font_size=22, color=ORANGE).next_to(energy_arrow, DOWN, buff=0.15)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(nucleus), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(Create(shell_inner), Create(shell_outer), FadeIn(electron), FadeIn(static_electron), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(energy_arrow), FadeIn(energy_text), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(electron, ArcBetweenPoints(shell_inner.point_at_angle(PI / 4), shell_outer.point_at_angle(PI / 3), angle=-PI / 3)), run_time=3)
        self.play(Indicate(shell_outer, color=TEAL_A), run_time=0.8)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that explains orbital phase by comparing two p orbitals at different orientations. Show colored lobes, rotate one orientation into another, and mention that sign and orientation matter.
completion:
from manim import *

class OrbitalPhaseRotation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Orbital Phase and Orientation", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        left_orbital = VGroup(
            Ellipse(width=1.2, height=2.1, stroke_color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_C, fill_opacity=0.32).shift(UP * 1.1),
            Ellipse(width=1.2, height=2.1, stroke_color=RED_D, fill_color=RED_C, fill_opacity=0.32).shift(DOWN * 1.1),
            DashedLine(LEFT * 1.6, RIGHT * 1.6, color=GRAY_C),
        ).move_to(LEFT * 3.1)
        right_orbital = left_orbital.copy().rotate(PI / 2).move_to(RIGHT * 3.1)
        left_label = Text("vertical orientation", font_size=22).next_to(left_orbital, DOWN)
        right_label = Text("horizontal orientation", font_size=22).next_to(right_orbital, DOWN)
        plus_minus = VGroup(
            MathTex("+", color=BLUE_D).move_to(left_orbital[0]),
            MathTex("-", color=RED_D).move_to(left_orbital[1]),
            MathTex("+", color=BLUE_D).move_to(right_orbital[0]),
            MathTex("-", color=RED_D).move_to(right_orbital[1]),
        )
        arrow = CurvedArrow(left_orbital.get_right() + RIGHT * 0.2, right_orbital.get_left() + LEFT * 0.2, angle=-PI / 2, color=YELLOW_D)
        caption = VGroup(
            Text("The sign pattern stays paired to the lobe orientation.", font_size=24),
            Text("Rotation changes the direction of overlap in bonding pictures.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_C),
        ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.16).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(left_orbital), FadeIn(left_label), FadeIn(plus_minus[:2]), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(arrow), TransformFromCopy(left_orbital, right_orbital), FadeIn(right_label), FadeIn(plus_minus[2:]), run_time=5)
        self.play(Rotate(right_orbital, angle=PI / 2), plus_minus[2:].animate.rotate(PI / 2, about_point=right_orbital.get_center()), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), Indicate(left_orbital, color=BLUE_A), Indicate(right_orbital, color=GREEN_A), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)
